full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What are good components to ablate in the Livni et al. RSVM2 framework? I need two independent components
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI suggested several ablation candidates: (1) the regularization form — compare ℓ₂,∞ vs Frobenius norm (tests the core theorem), (2) the σ selection method — heuristic vs cross-validation vs fixed values (tests the practical contribution), (3) the loss function — multiclass hinge vs other losses. It recommended the first two as the most meaningful since they directly test the paper's two main contributions.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Confirmed my choices: (1) ℓ₂,∞ vs Frobenius regularization, (2) σ heuristic vs cross-validation. These test the paper's two main claims
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: These directly test Theorem 3.1 (regularizer form) and Section 5 (σ heuristic)

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me set up a 5-fold cross-validation loop for σ selection in my ablation experiment
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI provided a cross-validation loop using sklearn's KFold: iterate over folds, train the model with each candidate σ value, record validation accuracy, and select the σ with the best average performance. It used a generic training function signature.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Used the basic CV loop structure but changed the σ grid to be multiples of the heuristic value instead of arbitrary values, and adapted it to work with my training function signature
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Standard cross-validation — we covered this in class

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me write clear interpretation paragraphs for my ablation results — I need 5-7 sentences each connecting results to the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI provided a paragraph structure: (1) state the experimental result numerically, (2) compare to the control/baseline, (3) explain what this means for the paper's claim, (4) note any caveats or surprises, (5) connect to the broader implications. No specific content was filled in.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Gave me a structure for interpretation paragraphs. I wrote the actual content based on my observed results
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Interpretations match my actual experimental outcomes
